metadata.actual_title: Suicidal Ideation and Behavior: Prospective Assessment of Occurrence in Clinical Trials
metadata.note: FILE MISLABELED — filename implies IND safety reporting; actual content is suicidal ideation/behavior prospective assessment guidance. Genuine new content: no prior extraction found in v2/v3/v4/v5.

rules.rule_text: The FDA recommends that sponsors prospectively assess the occurrence of suicidal ideation and behavior in clinical trials for drugs where suicidal ideation and behavior may be a product-class effect or where the drug affects neurological or psychiatric function.
rules.source_section: I. Introduction
rules.verbatim_quote: FDA recommends that sponsors prospectively assess the occurrence of suicidal ideation and behavior in clinical trials for drugs where suicidal ideation and behavior may be a product-class effect or where the drug affects neurological or psychiatric function.

rules.rule_text: The Columbia Suicide Severity Rating Scale (C-SSRS) is the FDA-recommended instrument for prospective assessment of suicidal ideation and behavior in clinical trials.
rules.source_section: III.A. General Recommendations — Recommended Instrument
rules.verbatim_quote: FDA recommends the Columbia Suicide Severity Rating Scale (C-SSRS) as the instrument for use in clinical trials.
exceptions: Alternative instruments may be acceptable if they meet specified criteria — see FDA-SIB-005

rules.rule_text: The C-SSRS classifies suicidal ideation and behavior using 11 preferred C-CASA categories: five levels of suicidal ideation (passive ideation; active ideation nonspecific; active ideation with method but no intent or plan; active ideation with method and intent but no plan; active ideation with method, intent, and plan), five levels of suicidal behavior (completed suicide; suicide attempt; interrupted attempt; aborted attempt; preparatory acts or behaviors), and one category of self-injurious behavior without suicidal intent.
rules.source_section: III.A. General Recommendations — Recommended Instrument; Appendix A
rules.verbatim_quote: The C-SSRS uses the following 11 preferred C-CASA categories

rules.rule_text: The C-SSRS screening version takes 1–2 minutes to administer for patients who answer all questions negatively; for patients who endorse suicidal ideation or behavior, the full administration takes less than 10 minutes.
rules.source_section: III.A. General Recommendations — Recommended Instrument
rules.verbatim_quote: The C-SSRS screening version takes approximately 1–2 minutes to administer for patients who answer all questions negatively. For patients who endorse suicidal ideation or behavior, the full administration takes less than 10 minutes.

rules.rule_text: Alternative instruments to the C-SSRS may be acceptable if they: (1) include all 11 C-CASA categories; (2) use definitions consistent with those in C-CASA; (3) use probes to assess for all categories; (4) integrate other information (e.g., case narratives) into the final classification; (5) allow for direct C-CASA category classification without additional processing; and (6) include a training program.
rules.source_section: III.A. General Recommendations — Criteria for Alternative Instruments
rules.verbatim_quote: An alternative instrument may be acceptable if it: (1) includes all 11 C-CASA categories; (2) uses definitions consistent with those in C-CASA; (3) uses probes to assess for all categories; (4) integrates other information (e.g., case narratives) into the final classification; (5) allows for direct C-CASA category classification without additional processing; and (6) includes a training program.

rules.rule_text: All discrete suicidal ideation and behavior events must be separately classified and recorded; multiple events occurring between visits must each be separately documented, not collapsed into a single entry.
rules.source_section: III.B. General Recommendations — Data Management
rules.verbatim_quote: All discrete events must be separately classified and recorded. Multiple events occurring between visits must each be separately documented.

rules.rule_text: For future meta-analyses, FDA will consider suicidal behavior and suicidal ideation separately; sponsors should structure data collection and reporting to support this separation.
rules.source_section: III.B. General Recommendations — Data Management
rules.verbatim_quote: For future meta-analyses, FDA will consider suicidal behavior and suicidal ideation separately.

rules.rule_text: Prospective assessment of suicidal ideation and behavior applies to all clinical trials once a drug has been deemed appropriate for such assessment; the assessment requirement is not limited to pivotal trials.
rules.source_section: III.C. General Recommendations — Specific Trial Considerations
rules.verbatim_quote: All trials after the drug has been deemed appropriate for such assessment.
exceptions: Single-dose healthy volunteer trials may omit assessment

rules.rule_text: Single-dose healthy volunteer trials may omit prospective suicidal ideation and behavior assessment.
rules.source_section: III.C. General Recommendations — Specific Trial Considerations
rules.verbatim_quote: Single-dose healthy volunteer trials can omit the assessment.

rules.rule_text: Microdose trials may omit prospective suicidal ideation and behavior assessment.
rules.source_section: III.C. General Recommendations — Specific Trial Considerations
rules.verbatim_quote: Microdose trials can omit the assessment.

rules.rule_text: Populations with severe cognitive impairment who cannot reliably self-report are exempt from the C-SSRS prospective assessment requirement; sponsors should document the rationale for exemption.
rules.source_section: III.C. General Recommendations — Specific Trial Considerations — Difficult Populations
rules.verbatim_quote: Difficult populations include those with severe cognitive impairment who cannot reliably self-report.

rules.rule_text: Young children who are unable to self-report are exempt from the C-SSRS prospective assessment requirement; sponsors should document the rationale for exemption.
rules.source_section: III.C. General Recommendations — Specific Trial Considerations — Difficult Populations
rules.verbatim_quote: Young children unable to self-report.

rules.rule_text: Baseline assessment using the C-SSRS must include a lifetime assessment of suicidal ideation and behavior prior to study drug initiation.
rules.source_section: III.C. General Recommendations — Timing
rules.verbatim_quote: Baseline (lifetime assessment) prior to study drug initiation.

rules.rule_text: C-SSRS assessment must be conducted at all planned clinical visits throughout the trial, not only at scheduled efficacy or safety visits.
rules.source_section: III.C. General Recommendations — Timing
rules.verbatim_quote: All planned clinical visits throughout the trial.

rules.rule_text: C-SSRS assessment must also be conducted at unplanned clinical visits (e.g., emergency room contacts, unscheduled safety visits) during the trial.
rules.source_section: III.C. General Recommendations — Timing
rules.verbatim_quote: Unplanned visits.

rules.rule_text: For inpatient settings, C-SSRS assessment should be conducted at symptom assessment times rather than at every nursing contact; the assessment schedule should be clinically appropriate and prospectively defined in the protocol.
rules.source_section: III.C. General Recommendations — Timing — Inpatient Settings
rules.verbatim_quote: Inpatient: symptom assessment times only.

rules.rule_text: For ongoing trials where prospective C-SSRS assessment was not originally planned, sponsors should discuss implementation with FDA; data from trials that are well along in execution may not be optimal for meta-analysis but still have value for patient protection.
rules.source_section: III.C. General Recommendations — Implementation in Ongoing Trials
rules.verbatim_quote: Discuss with FDA. Data from well-along trials not optimal for meta-analysis but still valuable for patient protection.

rules.rule_text: For large simple trials (LSTs) with thousands of participants and minimal site contact, sponsors should discuss with FDA the most feasible approach to implementing suicidal ideation and behavior assessment, which may differ from the standard visit-based protocol.
rules.source_section: III.C. General Recommendations — Large Simple Trials
rules.verbatim_quote: Large simple trials: discuss with FDA the most feasible approach.

rules.rule_text: Prospective C-SSRS assessment is MANDATORY for all drugs being developed for psychiatric indications, including all phases of clinical development and all populations studied.
rules.source_section: IV. Specific Indication Recommendations — Psychiatric Indications
rules.verbatim_quote: All drugs being developed for psychiatric indications: mandatory.

rules.rule_text: Prospective C-SSRS assessment is MANDATORY for all antiepileptic drugs (AEDs), including all phases of clinical development; this requirement stems from the 2008 FDA analysis of AED class-wide suicidality signal.
rules.source_section: IV. Specific Indication Recommendations — Antiepileptic Drugs
rules.verbatim_quote: All antiepileptic drugs: mandatory.

rules.rule_text: Prospective C-SSRS assessment is MANDATORY for all neurologic drugs that are CNS-active (both inpatient and outpatient trials), including multiple-dose Phase 1 trials in healthy volunteers.
rules.source_section: IV. Specific Indication Recommendations — Neurologic CNS-Active Drugs
rules.verbatim_quote: All neurologic drugs that are CNS-active (inpatient and outpatient, including multiple-dose Phase 1 healthy volunteer trials): mandatory.
exceptions: Single-dose healthy volunteer trials may omit

rules.rule_text: For drugs pharmacologically similar to antidepressants, antiepileptics, or CNS-active neurologic drugs, at minimum the C-SSRS must be used in Phase 2 and Phase 3 trials even if the indication is not psychiatric or neurologic.
rules.source_section: IV. Specific Indication Recommendations — Pharmacologically Similar Drugs
rules.verbatim_quote: Pharmacologically similar drugs: minimum requirement for Phase 2 and Phase 3 trials.

rules.rule_text: The background rate of suicidal ideation and behavior in the study population must be considered when designing the assessment protocol; drugs studied in populations with high baseline rates (e.g., treatment-resistant depression) require more frequent assessment intervals.
rules.source_section: III.C. General Recommendations — Specific Trial Considerations
rules.verbatim_quote: Background rate of suicidal ideation and behavior in the study population must be considered.

rules.rule_text: Passive suicidal ideation (C-CASA Category 5) is defined as: wishes to be dead or not alive anymore, or wishes to fall asleep and not wake up, but no thoughts of killing oneself.
rules.source_section: Appendix A — C-CASA Category Definitions
rules.verbatim_quote: Category 5 — Passive Suicidal Ideation: Wishes to be dead or not alive anymore, or wishes to fall asleep and not wake up, but no thoughts of killing oneself.

rules.rule_text: Self-injurious behavior without suicidal intent (C-CASA Category 11) is defined as: self-injurious behavior for which there is no suicidal intent; intent must be assessed and documented separately from the behavior itself; absence of suicidal intent must be confirmed, not assumed.
rules.source_section: Appendix A — C-CASA Category Definitions
rules.verbatim_quote: Category 11 — Self-Injurious Behavior, No Suicidal Intent: Self-injurious behavior for which there is no suicidal intent. Intent must be assessed and documented separately.

rules.rule_text: Completed suicide (C-CASA Category 1) is defined as: self-inflicted death with evidence (explicit or implicit) that the person intended to die; cases must be adjudicated and documented; any ambiguous death in the context of possible self-harm must be conservatively classified.
rules.source_section: Appendix A — C-CASA Category Definitions
rules.verbatim_quote: Category 1 — Completed Suicide: Self-inflicted death with evidence (explicit or implicit) that the person intended to die.

rules.rule_text: An interrupted suicide attempt (C-CASA Category 3) is defined as: a potentially self-injurious act that was interrupted by another person before harm occurred; this category must be distinguished from an aborted attempt (Category 4), which is self-interrupted before any potential for self-injury.
rules.source_section: Appendix A — C-CASA Category Definitions
rules.verbatim_quote: Category 3 — Interrupted Attempt: A potentially self-injurious act that was interrupted by another person before harm occurred. Distinguish from Category 4 (Aborted Attempt), which is self-interrupted.
